behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam (backoff schedule calculation from failure count and cap). MOCK-MISS: failure is in retry math/state progression, not inherently at a module boundary. Rejected higher level: behavior text is strictly about delay progression ('doubles... up to a configured maximum'), which can be validated without real HTTP/OS/model dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff policy function/object with attempt counts 1..N and a max cap; assert delays are exponential and then clamped, including edge cases (zero/negative config guards, cap boundary).

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between HTTP response classification and retry/backoff policy. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of classifier alone can miss wiring bugs (e.g., wrong status/header passed to policy). Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text is about identifying response + computing wait, which a stubbed in-process transport can exercise.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware with a stub transport returning 429 (and/or retry-after metadata) then success; use fake clock to assert computed delay fed into scheduler matches policy and only rate-limit responses trigger retry path.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam across retry scheduler, request re-dispatch, and response propagation. Failure appears when modules hand off request state/timing. Integration-brake: no real network/OS is required to verify resend and transparent return; stub transport suffices. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real HTTP conditions, only resend correctness after wait.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub transport that first returns rate-limit then success; advance fake timers past wait; assert second call payload/method/headers equal original request and `call()` resolves to the success body without exposing internal retry details.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between retry counter exhaustion and error-mapping layer. A unit test of error class alone misses handoff bugs where generic errors leak. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/model needed; deterministic stubs can force repeated rate-limit responses. Rejected System: behavior text is type/contract mapping at retry boundary, not real infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries (e.g., 3), stub transport to always return rate-limit, run `call()` with fake timer advancement, assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected typed fields and not generic error types.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface orchestration seam across retry state machine + quota tracker + termination condition. MOCK-MISS: local unit tests can miss cross-module loop/termination bugs. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess; in-process stubs plus controlled clock can expose infinite-loop risk. Rejected System: behavior text targets internal chain termination logic, not external infra faults.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end library call with stubbed always-rate-limited transport and fake clock; assert total attempts equals initial+maxRetries, final error surfaces, and test enforces upper time/attempt bound to fail if loop does not terminate.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam for shared in-process state under concurrency (caller -> quota bucket -> dispatcher). Failure emerges from interaction/race conditions, not single-function logic. Integration-brake: behavior explicitly says 'same process', disqualifying need for distributed/system infra; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/shared bucket, fire many concurrent `call()` promises with controlled stub transport; assert total admitted attempts respect one shared quota and per-caller behavior reflects global depletion, not per-caller independent quotas.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between queueing component and dispatcher under async scheduling. FIFO violations typically appear only when queue + scheduler interact. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required; deterministic in-process queue with fake clock/event-loop control is enough. Rejected System: behavior text is ordering policy in client internals, not external transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue multiple calls quickly while quota blocks dispatch; release capacity stepwise and assert dispatch order matches submission order (including near-simultaneous submissions) using instrumentation on stub transport call sequence.
